title: The rule of law in the besieged world
primary_topics: rule of law under global stress
Trump cases and US Supreme Court
sub_topics: South Africa v. Israel (Gaza) at ICJ
March 2022 ICJ interim order against Russia

stances.claim: Three judicial systems are simultaneously besieged: the Philippine legal system (ICC challenge), US courts (Trump cases), and the ICJ (Russia-Ukraine and South Africa-Israel genocide cases) — making 2024 a stress test of the rule of law worldwide.
stances.rhetorical_move: global-tableau framing organizing disparate cases under one thesis

stances.claim: The ICJ and ICC must not be confused — ICJ is the World Court for inter-state litigation and integral to the UN (193 members, including US and China); ICC was created by the Rome Statute and tries individuals for crimes against humanity (123 members, excluding US and China).

Russia did not obey the March 2022 ICJ interim order yet invoked the rule of law in accusing Ukraine of genocide to justify its invasion — exposing the cynical use of legal rhetoric to disguise rule-of-force action.

Israel's compliance with the ICJ's six commands by February 26 will be a test for the faith of the free world in the rule of law — if Israel disobeys and America and its allies fail to enforce, the global legal order is fundamentally weakened.
stances.rhetorical_move: rhetorical question + 'awaiting with bated breath' as expectation framing

stances.claim: If the Supreme Court delays Trump cases past November, all may be dismissed via incumbent-only presidential immunity — the timing question is potentially as decisive as the merits.
stances.rhetorical_move: structural-procedural prediction of judicial delay's substantive consequences

events: SCOTUS oral arguments on Trump disqualification (February 8, 2024)

signature_phrases: I will await with bated breath
the faith of the free world in the rule of law
register_markers: As a firm believer in the rule of law
Will Israel… Will America… Will Trump
decision_framework_signals: applies global-tableau lens — multiple jurisdictions under one rule-of-law stress test
frames compliance with international interim orders as the operational test of rule of law in geopolitics
uses timing-as-outcome analysis for Trump cases (delay = de facto immunity)
cross_references: my columns on Sept. 4, 2023 and Jan. 15, 2024
The rule of law in the South and North China Sea in a future column
